Summary: The Informatica Filter transformation has been successfully and accurately converted to a Snowflake dbt model.
Logic_Equivalence_finding: The filter condition correctly maps ISNULL and IIF logic to equivalent Snowflake SQL syntax.
Metadata_Consistency_finding: All ports defined in the XML transformation have been explicitly projected in the dbt model.
Naming_Conventions_finding: The dbt model correctly utilizes the 'int_' prefix for intermediate filter transformations.
Function_Mapping_finding: Informatica IIF and ISNULL functions are correctly mapped to Snowflake IFF and IS NULL operators.
Upstream_Referencing_finding: Upstream references point correctly to the expected expression transformation using the dbt ref() macro.

Summary: Lookup logic and connections have been successfully converted to dbt SQL with correct JOIN conditions and column mappings.

Function_Mapping_finding: No specific Informatica functions required mapping; standard SQL logic is appropriately applied.
Upstream_Referencing_finding: Upstream source and transformation models are properly referenced using standard dbt ref() and source() macros.

Summary: Source Qualifier logic converted successfully, but uses SELECT * which omits explicit column metadata mapping.

Function_Mapping_finding: No specific Informatica functions to map in this Source Qualifier logic.
Upstream_Referencing_finding: Upstream source correctly referenced using the dbt source() macro.
